survivors.greek_left: νῦν δὲ ἀπόδος τὴν γυναῖκα τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι προφήτης ἐστὶν
survivors.reveal_left: Now then, restore the wife to the man.
survivors.reveal_right: For he is a prophet.
survivors.reason: Both sides are self-standing predications: an imperative on the left ('restore the wife'), and a causal copular clause on the right ('he is a prophet'); the ὅτι-causal aligns with PTNK's own ὅτι-break practice elsewhere in this chapter (20:6, 20:11).

survivors.greek_left: καὶ τὸν υἱὸν δὲ τῆς παιδίσκης ταύτης, εἰς ἔθνος μέγα ποιήσω αὐτόν,
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι σπέρμα σόν ἐστιν.
survivors.reveal_left: And the son of this maidservant too — into a great nation I will make him.
survivors.reveal_right: For he is your seed.
survivors.reason: Greek-left has finite verb ποιήσω with subject/object complete; Greek-right is an independent causal ὅτι-clause with copula ἐστιν and predicate nominative — both atomic predications standing alone.

survivors.greek_left: ὅτι, τὰς ἑπτὰ ἀμνάδας ταύτας λήμψῃ παρ᾿ ἐμοῦ,
survivors.greek_right: ἵνα ὦσίν μοι εἰς μαρτύριον ὅτι ἐγὼ ὤρυξα τὸ φρέαρ τοῦτο.
survivors.hebrew_right: בַּעֲבוּר תִּהְיֶה־לִּי לְעֵדָה כִּי חָפַרְתִּי אֶת־הַבְּאֵר הַזֹּאת
survivors.reveal_left: That these seven ewes you will take from my hand.
survivors.reveal_right: In order that they may be for me as a witness that I dug this well.

survivors.greek_left: ἵνα ὦσίν μοι εἰς μαρτύριον
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι ἐγὼ ὤρυξα τὸ φρέαρ τοῦτο.
survivors.reveal_left: In order that they may be for me as a witness.
survivors.reveal_right: That I dug this well.
survivors.reason: Greek-left ἵνα-clause has finite ὦσίν and stands alone; Greek-right is a finite content/causal ὅτι-clause with subject ἐγώ + aorist ὤρυξα + object — atomic predication, parallel to other ὅτι-detachments in the chapter.

survivors.greek_left: διὰ τοῦτο ἐπωνόμασεν τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ τόπου ἐκείνου φρέαρ ὁρκισμοῦ,
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι ἐκεῖ ὤμοσαν ἀμφότεροι.
survivors.hebrew_left: עַל־כֵּן קָרָא לַמָּקוֹם הַהוּא בְּאֵר שָׁבַע
survivors.reveal_left: Therefore he called the name of that place 'Well of the Oath.'
survivors.reveal_right: Because there both of them swore.
survivors.reason: Greek-left has finite aorist ἐπωνόμασεν with full naming construction; Greek-right is an independent causal ὅτι-clause with finite ὤμοσαν, adverb, and subject — clean atomic predications on both sides, structurally parallel to the 21:13 ὅτι-break.

survivors.greek_left: καὶ ἀναβλέψας Ἁβραὰμ τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς αὐτοῦ εἶδεν
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ἰδοὺ κριὸς εἷς κατεχόμενος ἐν φυτῷ σαβεκ τῶν κεράτων
survivors.reveal_left: And Abraham, lifting up his eyes, saw — finite predication (εἶδεν) with circumstantial participle, stands alone.
survivors.reveal_right: And behold, one ram caught in a sabek-thicket by its horns — ἰδοὺ + nominative presentational predication, parallels the standalone ἰδοὺ-units already in PTNK (e.g., 22:7 ἰδοὺ τὸ πῦρ καὶ τὰ ξύλα).
survivors.reason: Greek finite verb εἶδεν closes the seeing-event; the following ἰδοὺ + nominative is a recognized atomic presentational predication (already an ATU pattern in PTNK 22:7). Both sides reveal as self-standing on the Greek bidirectional test.

survivors.greek_left: ἦ μὴν εὐλογῶν εὐλογήσω σὲ
survivors.greek_right: καὶ πληθύνων πληθυνῶ τὸ σπέρμα σου ὡς τοὺς ἀστέρας τοῦ οὐρανοῦ καὶ ὡς τὴν ἄμμον τὴν παρὰ τὸ χεῖλος τῆς θαλάσσης
survivors.hebrew_right: וְהַרְבָּה אַרְבֶּה אֶת־זַרְעֲךָ כְּכוֹכְבֵי הַשָּׁמַיִם וְכַחוֹל אֲשֶׁר עַל־שְׂפַת הַיָּם
survivors.reveal_left: Surely, blessing I will bless you — finite future predication with figura etymologica intensifier, atomic.
survivors.reveal_right: And multiplying I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven and as the sand on the seashore — finite future predication with figura etymologica + comparison adjuncts, atomic.
survivors.reason: Greek preserves the Hebrew figura-etymologica as two parallel finite-future predications joined by καί; each has its own subject-verb-object structure and reveals as a self-standing oath-clause on the Greek bidirectional test.

survivors.greek_left: ἐὰν δὲ μὴ θέλῃ ἡ γυνὴ πορευθῆναι μετὰ σου εἰς τὴν γῆν ταύτην,
survivors.greek_right: καθαρὸς ἔσῃ ἀπὸ τοῦ ὅρκου τούτου·
survivors.reveal_left: If the woman is not willing to come with you into this land
survivors.reveal_right: you shall be clean from this oath of mine
survivors.reason: Standard ἐάν protasis/apodosis: both halves are self-standing finite predications (conditional clause + main clause with copula+predicate adj.). Hebrew breaks the weqatal apodosis; Greek sustains the same split on the bidirectional test.

survivors.greek_left: εἰ οὖν ποιεῖτε ὑμεῖς ἔλεος καὶ δικαιοσύνην πρὸς τὸν κύριόν μου,
survivors.greek_right: ἀπαγγείλατε μοι,
survivors.reveal_left: If, then, you are dealing kindly and truly with my lord
survivors.reveal_right: tell me
survivors.reason: Conditional protasis + imperative apodosis — both atomic predications. PTNK already breaks the parallel negative branch (εἰ δὲ μή | ἀπαγγείλατε μοι) two lines later, so this is the same Greek pattern dropped inconsistently in the positive branch — a class-level under-segmentation.

survivors.greek_left: οὗτοί εἰσιν οἱ υἱοὶ Ἰσμαὴλ
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ταῦτα τὰ ὀνόματα αὐτῶν ἐν ταῖς σκηναῖς αὐτῶν· καὶ ἐν ταῖς ἐπαύλεσιν αὐτῶν
survivors.reveal_left: These are the sons of Ishmael.
survivors.reveal_right: And these are their names in their villages and in their encampments.
survivors.reason: Two parallel copular (verbless/εἰμί) predications joined only by paratactic καί; each is a syntactically complete and semantically self-standing demonstrative-copular clause in Greek; the conjunction carries zero force (predication boundary, not a comma).

survivors.greek_left: ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ πορεύομαι τελευτᾶν,
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ἵνα τί μοι ταῦτα τὰ πρωτοτόκια;
survivors.reveal_left: Behold, I am going to die.
survivors.reveal_right: And what use are these birthrights to me?
survivors.reason: Declarative finite predication (πορεύομαι + complementary infinitive) joined to an independent rhetorical interrogative (ἵνα τί + verbless predicate nominative ταῦτα τὰ πρωτοτόκια) by paratactic καί; both stand atomic on the Greek bidirectional test — a declaration and a rhetorical question are distinct idea-units.

survivors.greek_left: ἀνθ᾿ ὧν ὑπήκουσεν Ἁβραὰμ ὁ πατήρ σου τῆς ἐμῆς φωνῆς
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ἐφύλαξεν τὰ προστάγματά μου καὶ τὰς ἐντολάς μου καὶ τὰ δικαιώματά μου καὶ τὰ νόμιμά μου.
survivors.reveal_left: Because Abraham your father obeyed my voice.
survivors.reveal_right: And he kept my charges, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.
survivors.reason: Two independent finite-verb predications (ὑπήκουσεν / ἐφύλαξεν) joined only by καί; each reveals as a self-standing atomic idea-unit; PTNK glued them into a single line.

survivors.greek_left: μήποτε ἀποκτείνωσιν αὐτὸν οἱ ἄνδρες τοῦ τόπου περὶ Ῥεβέκκας,
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι ὡραία τῇ ὄψει ἦν.
survivors.reveal_left: Lest the men of the place kill him on account of Rebecca.
survivors.reveal_right: Because she was beautiful in appearance.
survivors.reason: Causal ὅτι-clause with its own finite verb (ἦν) is a complete predication; reveals atomically as standalone reason-statement separate from the fear-clause it grounds.

survivors.greek_left: ἄπελθε ἀφ᾿ ἡμῶν,
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι δυνατώτερος ἡμῶν ἐγένου σφόδρα.
survivors.reveal_left: Go away from us.
survivors.reveal_right: Because you have become much stronger than us.
survivors.reason: Imperative (ἄπελθε) and finite causal clause (ἐγένου) are each self-standing predications; the ὅτι-clause is not a syntactic complement of the imperative but a discourse-level grounding.

survivors.greek_left: καὶ ἐξαπεστείλαμέν σὲ μετ᾿ εἰρήνης·
survivors.greek_right: καὶ νῦν σὺ εὐλογητὸς ὑπὸ κυρίου.
survivors.reveal_left: And we sent you away in peace.
survivors.reveal_right: And now you are blessed by the Lord.
survivors.reason: Past-tense narrative (ἐξαπεστείλαμεν) and a present nominal/equational predication (σὺ εὐλογητός) with explicit subject and discourse marker καὶ νῦν; two distinct atomic idea-units glued by PTNK into one long line.

survivors.greek_left: οὐκ ἔστιν τοῦτο ἀλλ᾿ ἢ οἶκος θεοῦ
survivors.greek_right: καὶ αὕτη ἡ πύλη τοῦ οὐρανοῦ
survivors.reveal_left: This is nothing other than the house of God.
survivors.reveal_right: And this is the gate of heaven.
survivors.reason: Both sides are self-standing nominal predications (left: explicit ἔστιν with τοῦτο subject; right: αὕτη subject + ἡ πύλη τοῦ οὐρανοῦ predicate nominative with elided copula). The καί + new demonstrative αὕτη marks a fresh predication parallel to τοῦτο, exactly as Hebrew זֶה...וְזֶה does. CATSS confirms 1:1 word alignment at the break.

survivors.greek_left: ὄνομα τῇ μείζονι Λεία
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ὄνομα τῇ νεωτέρᾳ Ῥαχήλ
survivors.reveal_left: The name of the elder was Leah.
survivors.reveal_right: And the name of the younger was Rachel.
survivors.reason: Two parallel verbless naming predications (subject + predicate-nominative), each grammatically and semantically self-standing; PTNK merges with comma.

survivors.greek_left: οἱ δὲ ὀφθαλμοὶ Λείας ἀσθενεῖς
survivors.greek_right: Ῥαχὴλ δὲ καλὴ τῷ εἴδει καὶ ὡραία τῇ ὄψει
survivors.reveal_left: But Leah's eyes were weak.
survivors.reveal_right: But Rachel was beautiful in form and lovely in appearance.
survivors.reason: Two contrasting stative predications about different subjects (Leah / Rachel), joined by δέ-contrast; each is independently atomic. PTNK fuses both descriptions on one line.

survivors.greek_left: ἀνάβλεψον τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς σου
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ἰδὲ τοὺς τράγους καὶ τοὺς κριοὺς ἀναβαίνοντας ἐπὶ τὰ πρόβατα καὶ τὰς αἶγας διαλεύκους καὶ ποικίλους καὶ σποδοειδεῖς ῥαντούς
survivors.hebrew_right: וּרְאֵה֙ כָּל־הָֽעַתֻּדִים֙ הָעֹלִ֣ים עַל־הַצֹּ֔אן עֲקֻדִּ֥ים נְקֻדִּ֖ים וּבְרֻדִּ֑ים
survivors.reveal_left: Lift up your eyes!
survivors.reveal_right: And see the goats and the rams going up upon the sheep and the goats — dappled, spotted and ash-grey speckled.
survivors.reason: Two coordinate imperatives joined by καί; each reveals as a self-standing imperative predication, no subordination, parallel to the established 'ἀνάστηθι καὶ ἔξελθε καὶ ἄπελθε' pattern PTNK already splits in v13.

survivors.greek_left: τί τὸ ἀδίκημά μου
survivors.greek_right: καὶ τί τὸ ἁμάρτημά μου, ὅτι κατεδίωξας ὀπίσω μου
survivors.reveal_left: What is my offence?
survivors.reveal_right: And what is my sin, that you have pursued after me?
survivors.reason: Two parallel verbless interrogative clauses (τί + nominative subject), each a complete predication; the ὅτι-clause attaches naturally to the second question alone.

survivors.greek_left: αἱ θυγατέρες θυγατέρες μου
survivors.greek_right: καὶ οἱ υἱοὶ υἱοί μου, καὶ τὰ κτήνη κτήνη μου

survivors.reveal_right: And the sons are my sons, and the flocks are my flocks.
survivors.reason: Triple parallel verbless possession clauses (subject = predicate-nominative); each is a freestanding nominal predication. Greek mirrors the Hebrew tricolon with the same article+noun+noun-MOU pattern.

survivors.greek_left: καὶ οἱ υἱοὶ υἱοί μου
survivors.greek_right: καὶ τὰ κτήνη κτήνη μου
survivors.reveal_left: And the sons are my sons.
survivors.reveal_right: And the flocks are my flocks.
survivors.reason: Second internal break of the tricolon; each colon is an atomic verbless nominal clause.

survivors.greek_left: οὕτως ἐρεῖτε τῷ κυρίῳ μου Ἡσαῦ,
survivors.greek_right: οὕτως λέγει ὁ παῖς σου Ἰακώβ,
survivors.reveal_left: Thus you shall say to my lord Esau —
survivors.reveal_right: Thus says your servant Jacob —
survivors.reason: Frame-on-frame: outer command-to-deliver (οὕτως ἐρεῖτε) and inner messenger-formula (οὕτως λέγει) are each atomic frame predications, each with its own verb of speaking + addressee/speaker. Standard ATU class (matrix frame | embedded frame).

survivors.greek_left: ἐρεῖς,
survivors.greek_right: τοῦ παιδὸς σου Ἰακώβ· δῶρα ἀπέσταλκεν τῷ κυρίῳ μου Ἡσαῦ,
survivors.hebrew_right: לְעַבְדְּךָ לְיַעֲקֹב מִנְחָה הִוא שְׁלוּחָה לַאדֹנִי לְעֵשָׂו
survivors.reveal_left: You shall say —
survivors.reveal_right: [From] your servant Jacob: he has sent gifts to my lord Esau.
survivors.reason: Bare frame ἐρεῖς is atomic (standard frame|quote class; PTNK applies this break elsewhere in chapter, e.g., 32:8, 32:9). Right side is a complete message with finite verb ἀπέσταλκεν.

survivors.greek_left: ἐρεῖς, τοῦ παιδὸς σου Ἰακώβ· δῶρα ἀπέσταλκεν τῷ κυρίῳ μου Ἡσαῦ,
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ἰδοὺ αὐτὸς ὀπίσω ἡμῶν.
survivors.hebrew_left: וְאָמַרְתָּ לְעַבְדְּךָ לְיַעֲקֹב מִנְחָה הִוא שְׁלוּחָה לַאדֹנִי לְעֵשָׂו
survivors.reveal_left: You shall say, '[From] your servant Jacob: he has sent gifts to my lord Esau.'
survivors.reveal_right: And behold, he himself is behind us.
survivors.reason: Presentational ἰδού + nominal predication with implicit copula is a standalone atomic deictic clause (αὐτὸς is subject, ὀπίσω ἡμῶν predicate). Topic shifts from sending-of-gifts (past) to presence-of-Jacob (present). Discrete predication on each side.

survivors.greek_left: καὶ λυπηρὸν ἦν αὐτοῖς σφόδρα ὅτι ἄσχημον ἐποίησεν ἐν Ἰσραὴλ κοιμηθεὶς μετὰ τῆς θυγατρὸς Ἰακώβ
survivors.greek_right: καὶ οὐχ οὕτως ἔσται
survivors.reveal_left: and it was very grievous to them that he had done a shameful thing in Israel by lying with the daughter of Jacob
survivors.reveal_right: and such a thing shall not be done
survivors.reason: καί + future-indicative ἔσται is a coordinated independent finite predication, not subordinate to the prior ὅτι-clause (which closes at Ἰακώβ); both halves stand as self-contained idea-units (a complaint, then a normative judgment).

survivors.greek_left: καὶ ἐν ἡμῖν κατοικεῖτε
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ἡ γῆ ἰδοὺ πλατεῖα ἐναντίον ὑμῶν
survivors.reveal_left: and dwell with us
survivors.reveal_right: and behold, the land is wide before you
survivors.reason: Left = imperative κατοικεῖτε with prepositional adjunct, complete. Right = independent nominal/verbal predication with subject ἡ γῆ + predicate adjective πλατεῖα. Both are atomic; the comma in the PTNK line is the symptom, the predication shift is the cause.

survivors.greek_left: οὐ δυνησόμεθα ποιῆσαι τὸ ῥῆμα τοῦτο, δοῦναι τὴν ἀδελφὴν ἡμῶν ἀνθρώπῳ, ὃς ἔχει ἀκροβυστίαν
survivors.greek_right: ἔστιν γὰρ ὄνειδος ἡμῖν
survivors.hebrew_left: לֹא נוּכַל לַעֲשׂוֹת הַדָּבָר הַזֶּה לָתֵת אֶת־אֲחֹתֵנוּ לְאִישׁ אֲשֶׁר־לוֹ עָרְלָה
survivors.reveal_left: we cannot do this thing, to give our sister to a man who has uncircumcision
survivors.reveal_right: for it is a reproach to us
survivors.reason: γάρ is a postpositive coordinating particle; ἔστιν γὰρ ὄνειδος ἡμῖν is a complete independent finite predication supplying the ground, not a syntactic subordinate of the refusal. Standard ground-clause split.

survivors.greek_left: θάρσει,
survivors.greek_right: καὶ γὰρ οὗτός Σοὶ ἐστιν υἱός.
survivors.reveal_left: Take courage! (atomic imperative predication, self-standing exhortation from the midwife)
survivors.reveal_right: For this one too is a son for you. (atomic predication: finite ἐστιν, complete subject οὗτος + predicate υἱός; γάρ-causal that stands as its own idea-unit)
survivors.reason: PTNK folds imperative + its causal explanation into one Greek line; the imperative θάρσει and the causal ἐστιν-clause each pass the Greek bidirectional test independently — two distinct atomic predications (encouragement + reason) sharing only the γάρ link.

survivors.greek_left: οὗτοι υἱοὶ Ἡσαῦ,
survivors.greek_right: καὶ οὗτοι ἡγεμόνες αὐτῶν.
survivors.reveal_left: These are the sons of Esau.
survivors.reveal_right: And these are their chiefs.
survivors.reason: Two parallel nominal clauses each with explicit demonstrative subject (οὗτοι) and predicate nominative; each is a complete predication standing alone, and the second has its own subject οὗτοι rather than continuing the first.

survivors.greek_left: καὶ ἐβασίλευσεν ἐν Ἐδὼμ Βαλὰκ υἱὸς τοῦ Βεώρ,
survivors.greek_right: καὶ ὄνομα τῇ πόλει αὐτοῦ Δενναβά.
survivors.reveal_left: And Balak son of Beor reigned in Edom.
survivors.reveal_right: And the name of his city was Dennaba.
survivors.reason: Finite-verb reign clause and an independent nominal city-name clause (subject ὄνομα, predicate nominative Δενναβά). The identical pattern is already broken by PTNK at 36:35; failing to break it here is internally inconsistent with PTNK's own segmentation of the king-list formula.

survivors.greek_left: καὶ ὄνομα τῇ πόλει αὐτοῦ Φογώρ,
survivors.greek_right: ὄνομα δὲ τῇ γυναικὶ αὐτοῦ Μαιτεβεὴλ θυγάτηρ Ματραὶθ υἱοῦ Μαιζοόβ.
survivors.hebrew_right: וְשֵׁם אִשְׁתּוֹ מְהֵיטַבְאֵל בַּת־מַטְרֵד בַּת מֵי זָהָב
survivors.reveal_left: And the name of his city was Phogor.
survivors.reveal_right: And the name of his wife was Maitebeel, daughter of Matraith son of Maizoob.
survivors.reason: Two independent nominal clauses with parallel structure (ὄνομα-subject + dative-of-relation + predicate-nominative proper name). The second introduces a new topic (wife, with δέ marking the topic shift), not a continuation of the city-name clause.

survivors.greek_left: Ἰακὼβ δὲ ἠγάπα τὸν Ἰωσὴφ παρὰ πάντας τοὺς υἱοὺς αὐτοῦ
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι υἱὸς γήρους ἦν αὐτῷ
survivors.reveal_left: Jacob loved Joseph above all his sons.
survivors.reveal_right: Because he was a son of old age to him.
survivors.reason: ὅτι causal clause with finite verb ἦν has a fully independent predication; the main clause is also a complete finite predication. Both reveal atomically without dependence on the other.

survivors.greek_left: καὶ ἐροῦμεν
survivors.greek_right: Θηρίον πονηρὸν κατέφαγεν αὐτόν
survivors.reveal_left: And we will say.
survivors.reveal_right: A wild beast has devoured him.
survivors.reason: Speech-frame (finite ἐροῦμεν) and speech-content (finite κατέφαγεν, complete S+V+O) are two distinct atomic predications; the direct quote is independently revealable. PTNK joins them by comma within one line.

survivors.greek_left: αἱ δὲ χεῖρες ἡμῶν μὴ ἔστωσαν ἐπ᾿ αὐτόν
survivors.greek_right: ὅτι ἀδελφὸς ἡμῶν καὶ σὰρξ ἡμῶν ἐστιν
survivors.reveal_left: But let our hands not be upon him.
survivors.reveal_right: Because he is our brother and our flesh.
survivors.reason: Causal ὅτι clause with finite ἐστιν is a complete independent predication; the prohibitive main clause (ἔστωσαν) is a complete finite predication. Both reveal atomically. Same rule-class as 37:3 (ὅτι-causal-with-finite-VP).

survivors.greek_left: ἐπηρώτησεν δὲ τοὺς ἄνδρας τοὺς ἐκ τοῦ τόπου
survivors.greek_right: Ποῦ ἐστιν ἡ πόρνη ἡ γενομένη ἐν Αἰνὰν ἐπὶ τῆς ὁδοῦ;
survivors.reveal_left: And he asked the men of the place
survivors.reveal_right: Where is the prostitute who was at Aenan on the road?
survivors.reason: Standard speech-frame | direct-quote boundary. Left is an atomic finite reporting clause; right is an atomic direct interrogative. PTNK breaks this pattern at every other quote in the chapter (vv. 8, 13, 16, 17, 18, 22, 23, 24, 25, 28, 29) — a real under-segmentation.

survivors.greek_left: Οὐχ εὗρον,
survivors.greek_right: καὶ οἱ ἄνθρωποι οἱ ἐκ τοῦ τόπου λέγουσιν μὴ εἶναι ὧδε πόρνην.
survivors.hebrew_right: וְגַם אַנְשֵׁי הַמָּקוֹם אָמְרוּ לֹא־הָיְתָה בָזֶה קְדֵשָׁה
